reasons: Closed-loop contract → receipt → gate chain is complete and verifiable
All claims have EvidenceRef support with file paths and line numbers
No unproven stability claims: template is definition-only, not execution
No permit/gate bypass: template enforces review/compliance local to LOCAL-ANTIGRAVITY
Time window compliant: execution completed within 2026-03-07 dispatch window
Artifact integrity (N2): all required artifacts present (template, execution_report, completion_record)
Command allowlist compliant (N1): template only references existing lobsterctl and aggregation scripts
Three-tier separation enforced: cannot_approve flag + location constraint
No executor self-approval path exists in template design
Template structure validated against reference implementations (L4-n8n evidence pattern)
Remaining risks documented with mitigation strategies
Out of scope items clearly documented (no direct cloud launch, no infrastructure changes)

evidence_refs.description: Execution receipt with deliverables and completion status
Three-tier role separation with cannot_approve enforcement
Scope completed with EvidenceRef for each claim
Exit gate decision with ALLOW outcome

execution_report.verification: File exists and is complete (100 lines)

compliance_attestation.message: T3-C batch template design is compliant with Antigravity-1 requirements. Closed-loop complete, no unproven claims, no bypasses, all artifacts present.
compliance_attestation.authorized_for: Template use in M2/M3/M4 batch dispatch (pending separate activation authorization)
compliance_attestation.not_authorized_for: Direct cloud batch launch (requires separate authorization)

notes: T3-C is a template definition task only. The template itself is compliant and ready for use. However, using the template to launch actual cloud batches requires separate activation and authorization. The template enforces all Antigravity-1 requirements including three-tier separation, local review/compliance, and evidence traceability.
